summary: A wildly energetic SPAC show with a standout Scarlet > Fire and epic Wheel‑Playin’‑Morning Dew, captured on an audience tape that’s listenable though not pristine.
review: The performance is hailed as one of the best from the early‑80s era – Jerry’s guitar roars through Scarlet > Fire, the Wheel into a soaring Playin’ reprise and a transcendent Morning Dew, with Althea and the Wheel also getting high marks.  The audience recording (Sennheiser 441 tape) is generally clear enough to enjoy the crowd ambience, though a few reviewers note it’s only “average” or “nice for an aud”.  Overall the musical highs outweigh the occasional flubs (e.g., Jack Straw), making this a positive listening experience.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4‑5 stars and praise the musical performance; recording quality is consistently described as good/average but still enjoyable, yielding a strong combined score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Audience tape recorded with Sennheiser 441 mics; clear enough to hear instruments and crowd, occasional muddiness or average sound noted, but overall listenable and captures the SPAC ambience.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set solid with a few loose moments (e.g., Jack Straw), but the second set shines – seamless transitions from Scarlet into Fire, then a magical Wheel‑Playin’‑Dew sequence that many reviewers call the highlight of the tour.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as having huge energy and a powerful guitar tone, especially during Scarlet > Fire and Althea.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass work on Morning Dew praised as “blowing everything away” with deep, resonant double‑stop bombs.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboards noted as “badass” and adding depth to the jams; Keith isn’t mentioned directly.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill & Mickey get credit for tight timing on the Scarlet > Fire transition and overall solid drumming throughout.
